sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Adobe Analytics connector** integrates with the Adobe Analytics 2.0 API. It enables Shielva to sync report suites, segments, and calculated metrics from your Adobe Analytics organization into the knowledge base.

This connector uses **OAuth2 client_credentials grant** via Adobe IMS. The access token is acquired automatically and refreshed before expiry.

**Key capabilities:**
- Authenticate via OAuth2 client_credentials (Adobe IMS)
- Sync all report suites for the company
- Sync segments and calculated metrics
- Run custom reports (ranked, trended) via POST /reports
- List dimensions and metrics per report suite
- Stable document IDs via `SHA-256('report_suite:'+rsid)[:16]` for deduplication
- Automatic token refresh (24h TTL, 30s pre-expiry buffer)
- Automatic retry with exponential back-off on transient failures

children.title: Connector Type
children.content: `adobe_analytics` — registered in ACP as **Adobe Analytics**.

children.title: Provider
children.content: Adobe Inc. (Adobe Analytics 2.0 API — `https://analytics.adobe.io/api/{company_id}/...`)

children.title: Supported Operations
children.content:
| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `install()` | Validates credentials + acquires OAuth2 token; returns HEALTHY/CONNECTED or OFFLINE |
| `health_check()` | Token + report suite list; returns suite count in message |
| `sync()` | Fetches report suites + segments + calculated metrics; normalizes to ConnectorDocuments |
| `list_report_suites()` | GET /reportsuites — all report suites for the company |
| `list_dimensions(rsid)` | GET /dimensions?rsid= — all dimensions for a report suite |
| `list_metrics(rsid)` | GET /metrics?rsid= — all metrics for a report suite |
| `list_segments(rsid)` | GET /segments?rsid= — all segments for a report suite |
| `run_report(rsid, metrics, dimensions)` | POST /reports — run a ranked report |

sections.title: Authentication

children.title: Auth Type
children.content: `oauth2` (server-to-server client_credentials grant). Credentials are created in Adobe Developer Console → Projects → OAuth Server-to-Server.

children.title: Install Fields
children.content:
| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| Client ID (API Key) | `client_id` | string | Yes | OAuth2 Client ID from Adobe Developer Console |
| Client Secret | `client_secret` | password | Yes | OAuth2 Client Secret |
| Global Company ID | `company_id` | string | Yes | Short alphanumeric slug used in all API URLs |
| Organization ID | `organization_id` | string | No | IMS Org ID (format: {hex}@AdobeOrg) |

children.title: Token Details
children.content:
| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Token URL | `https://ims-na1.adobelogin.com/ims/token/v3` |
| Grant type | `client_credentials` |
| Scopes | `openid,AdobeID,read_organizations,additional_info.projectedProductContext,additional_info.job_function` |
| TTL | 24 hours (86399s from Adobe) |
| Refresh buffer | 30 seconds before expiry |

Tokens are stored in process memory only — never written to disk or logs.

children.title: Credential Security
children.content: Client secrets are stored AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest by the Shielva vault. Neither `client_secret` nor `access_token` values ever appear in log output. The connector uses read-only API scopes — it cannot modify Adobe Analytics data.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content: All configuration is stored in the connector's `config` dict (encrypted at rest by Shielva vault).

children.title: Config Keys
children.content:
| Key | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `client_id` | Adobe Analytics OAuth2 Client ID (API Key) |
| `client_secret` | Adobe Analytics OAuth2 Client Secret |
| `company_id` | Global Company ID — used in all API URL paths |
| `organization_id` | IMS Org ID (optional, informational) |

children.title: API Base URL
children.content:
All Analytics 2.0 API calls use:
```
https://analytics.adobe.io/api/{company_id}/{resource}
```
The `company_id` is filled from the connector config. Token requests always go to:
```
https://ims-na1.adobelogin.com/ims/token/v3
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All methods are implemented in `connector.py` and delegate HTTP calls to `AdobeAnalyticsHTTPClient` in `client/http_client.py`.

children.title: install()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def install(self) -> InstallResult`

Validates credentials and establishes the connector.

**Steps:**
1. Check `client_id` non-empty → MISSING_CREDENTIALS if not.
2. Check `client_secret` non-empty → MISSING_CREDENTIALS if not.
3. Check `company_id` non-empty → MISSING_CREDENTIALS if not.
4. Call `get_token()` via `with_retry`.
5. On success, create fresh http_client.

**Returns:**
- `InstallResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='Connected to Adobe Analytics')`
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` — missing field
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` — 401/403
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=FAILED)` — network/server error

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def health_check(self) -> HealthCheckResult`

Acquires token and lists report suites to verify connectivity.

**Returns:**
- `HealthCheckResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='Connected to Adobe Analytics (2 report suite(s))')`
- `HealthCheckResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` on 401/403
- `HealthCheckResult(health=DEGRADED, auth_status=FAILED)` on network error

children.title: sync()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def sync(self, full=False, since=None, kb_id='') -> SyncResult`

Full sync across three data categories:
1. **Report suites** — `GET /reportsuites` → all suites normalized to ConnectorDocuments
2. **Segments** — `GET /segments?rsid={first_rsid}` → segments for first report suite
3. **Calculated metrics** — `GET /calculatedmetrics` → all calculated metrics

Segment and calculated metric failures are non-fatal.

**Returns:** `SyncResult` with documents_found, documents_synced, documents_failed.
Status: `COMPLETED` when no failures; `PARTIAL` when some fail or zero docs found.

children.title: list_report_suites()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_report_suites(self) -> list[dict]`

Returns all report suites for the company.

**Returns:** List of report suite dicts with rsid, name, currency, timezoneZoneinfo, status.

children.title: list_dimensions(report_suite_id)
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_dimensions(self, report_suite_id: str) -> list[dict]`

Returns all dimensions for a report suite.

**Parameters:**
- `report_suite_id` — RSID string (e.g. 'mycompany.prod')

**Returns:** List of dimension dicts with id, name, type.

children.title: list_metrics(report_suite_id)
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_metrics(self, report_suite_id: str) -> list[dict]`

Returns all metrics for a report suite.

**Parameters:**
- `report_suite_id` — RSID string

**Returns:** List of metric dicts with id, name, type.

children.title: list_segments(report_suite_id)
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_segments(self, report_suite_id: str) -> list[dict]`

Returns all segments for a report suite.

**Parameters:**
- `report_suite_id` — RSID string

**Returns:** List of segment dicts with id, name, description, owner, tags.

children.title: run_report(report_suite_id, metrics, dimensions, date_range)
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def run_report(self, report_suite_id: str, metrics: list[str], dimensions: list[str], date_range: str = '') -> dict`

Runs a ranked Adobe Analytics report.

**Parameters:**
- `report_suite_id` — RSID string
- `metrics` — list of metric IDs (e.g. `['metrics/visits', 'metrics/pageviews']`)
- `dimensions` — list of dimension IDs (e.g. `['variables/page']`); first one used
- `date_range` — optional ISO-8601 date range string

**Returns:** Raw JSON report response with `rows` and `columns`.

sections.title: HTTP Client
sections.content: All HTTP calls are encapsulated in `AdobeAnalyticsHTTPClient` (`client/http_client.py`) using `aiohttp.ClientSession`. The connector never calls HTTP directly.

children.title: Methods
children.content:
| Method | Adobe Analytics API Call | Description |
|--------|------------------------|-------------|
| `get_token()` | `POST https://ims-na1.adobelogin.com/ims/token/v3` | Acquire OAuth2 access token |
| `get_report_suites()` | `GET /api/{company_id}/reportsuites` | All report suites |
| `get_dimensions(rsid)` | `GET /api/{company_id}/dimensions?rsid=` | Dimensions for a report suite |
| `get_metrics(rsid)` | `GET /api/{company_id}/metrics?rsid=` | Metrics for a report suite |
| `run_report(rsid, body)` | `POST /api/{company_id}/reports` | Run a ranked report |
| `get_segments(rsid)` | `GET /api/{company_id}/segments?rsid=` | Segments for a report suite |
| `get_calculated_metrics()` | `GET /api/{company_id}/calculatedmetrics` | All calculated metrics |

children.title: Error Mapping
children.content:
HTTP status codes are mapped to typed exceptions:

| HTTP Status | Exception |
|-------------|----------|
| 400 (token) | `AdobeAnalyticsAuthError` |
| 401 | `AdobeAnalyticsAuthError` |
| 403 | `AdobeAnalyticsAuthError` |
| 404 | `AdobeAnalyticsNotFoundError` |
| 429 | `AdobeAnalyticsRateLimitError` (with retry_after) |
| Connection/timeout | `AdobeAnalyticsNetworkError` |
| 5xx | `AdobeAnalyticsError` |
| Other 4xx | `AdobeAnalyticsError` |

children.title: Retry Logic
children.content:
`with_retry()` in `helpers/utils.py` wraps API calls with exponential back-off:
- `max_attempts=3` (configurable)
- Back-off: base 1s × 2^attempt + random jitter (0–0.5s), capped at 30s
- Skips retry on `AdobeAnalyticsAuthError` — re-installing is required
- For `AdobeAnalyticsRateLimitError`: honours `retry_after` if > 0, otherwise back-off formula
- Retries on all other `AdobeAnalyticsError` and `AdobeAnalyticsNetworkError` subclasses

sections.title: Data Model
sections.content: Normalized documents produced by the helper functions in `helpers/utils.py`.

children.title: Report Suite ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "source_id": "<sha256('report_suite:mycompany.prod')[:16]>",
  "title": "Adobe Analytics report suite: Production Site",
  "content": "Report Suite ID: mycompany.prod\nName: Production Site\nCurrency: USD\nTimezone: America/New_York\nStatus: active",
  "source_url": "https://analytics.adobe.com",
  "metadata": {
    "rsid": "mycompany.prod",
    "name": "Production Site",
    "currency": "USD",
    "timezone": "America/New_York",
    "status": "active",
    "type": "report_suite"
  }
}
```

children.title: Segment ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "source_id": "<sha256('segment:s300012345_5f123abc')[:16]>",
  "title": "Adobe Analytics segment: Mobile Users",
  "content": "Segment ID: s300012345_5f123abc\nName: Mobile Users\nDescription: All visits from mobile devices\nOwner: John Doe\nTags: mobile, devices",
  "source_url": "https://analytics.adobe.com",
  "metadata": {
    "id": "s300012345_5f123abc",
    "name": "Mobile Users",
    "description": "All visits from mobile devices",
    "owner": "John Doe",
    "tags": ["mobile", "devices"],
    "type": "segment"
  }
}
```

children.title: Calculated Metric ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "source_id": "<sha256('calculated_metric:cm300012345_5fabc123')[:16]>",
  "title": "Adobe Analytics calculated metric: Bounce Rate",
  "content": "Calculated Metric ID: cm300012345_5fabc123\nName: Bounce Rate\nDescription: Percentage of single-page visits\nFormula: metric('bounces')/metric('entries')\nOwner: Analytics Team\nPolarity: negative\nPrecision: 1",
  "source_url": "https://analytics.adobe.com",
  "metadata": {
    "id": "cm300012345_5fabc123",
    "name": "Bounce Rate",
    "formula": "metric('bounces')/metric('entries')",
    "owner": "Analytics Team",
    "type": "calculated_metric"
  }
}
```

children.title: Document ID Stability
children.content:
Document IDs are stable across syncs:
- Report suite: `SHA-256('report_suite:' + rsid)[:16]`
- Segment: `SHA-256('segment:' + id)[:16]`
- Calculated metric: `SHA-256('calculated_metric:' + id)[:16]`

The same Adobe Analytics data always produces the same Shielva document ID, enabling upsert deduplication without storing seen-ID lists.

sections.title: Exception Hierarchy
sections.content:
All exceptions inherit from `AdobeAnalyticsError` (base) in `exceptions.py`:

```
AdobeAnalyticsError (base)
├── AdobeAnalyticsAuthError         — 400/401/403 responses (token or API)
├── AdobeAnalyticsNetworkError      — connectivity failures, timeouts
├── AdobeAnalyticsNotFoundError     — 404 Not Found
└── AdobeAnalyticsRateLimitError    — 429 Too Many Requests (has retry_after attr)
```

Callers can catch the base `AdobeAnalyticsError` or specific subclasses for finer control.

children.title: Common Errors
children.content:
| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| `client_id is required` | Missing field | Enter the Client ID in the install form |
| `client_secret is required` | Missing field | Enter the Client Secret |
| `company_id is required` | Missing field | Enter the Global Company ID |
| `Authentication failed: invalid_client` | Wrong client_id or secret | Re-copy from Adobe Developer Console |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Token rejected | Verify credentials in Developer Console |
| `403 Forbidden` | No product profile access | Add product profile in Adobe Admin Console |
| `404 Not Found` | Wrong company_id | Verify via discovery API |
| `429 Too Many Requests` | Rate limit hit | Connector retries automatically |
| `500 Server Error` | Adobe API outage | Check status.adobe.com |

children.title: Testing
children.content:
Run the full test suite (60+ tests, all mocked — no live credentials needed):

```bash
cd /Users/vivekvarshavaishvik/Documents/client_dir/adobe_analytics_connector
source "/Volumes/V3-SSD/Shielva Project Dirs/.venv/bin/activate"
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

All tests must pass before registering the connector.
